summary: CC-0087 replaces an unfiltered keystonev1alpha1.KeystoneList call in secretToKeystoneMapper with a controller-runtime FieldIndexer lookup keyed by KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey = "spec.secretRefs.name". The extractor deduplicates and skips empty names; registration is error-wrapped with the index key; SetupWithManager propagates registration failure; the owner-reference fallback for rotation staging Secrets (CC-0081) is preserved and now matches on Kind+APIVersion; results are deduplicated by types.NamespacedName. Test coverage is excellent: extractor-level unit tests, mapper-level tests using a fake client with WithIndex + an interceptor.Funcs List recorder that asserts MatchingFields shape and absence of unfiltered Lists, three envtest integration tests exercising the positive reconcile path, negative non-match Consistently check, and duplicate-registration error surface. Production wiring path and test wiring path share the same registerSecretNameIndex helper, eliminating drift. All unit tests pass (go test ./... ok 1.337s) and integration tests pass with envtest (23.4s). go vet is clean; no new golangci-lint issues in the diff. Minor: the 1s Consistently window in the negative integration test is flake-prone on slow CI but acknowledged via inline DECISION comment. No blocking or major issues.

tests.T4_tests_are_deterministic: mostly-true; TestIntegration_UnrelatedSecretDoesNotTriggerReconcile uses 1s Consistently which could flake on overloaded CI — acknowledged in-code

code_quality.Q4_no_magic_numbers_or_strings: mostly-true; "Keystone" Kind literal is consistent with 6 other occurrences across the package — not worth extracting solely for this diff

suggested_improvements.observation: The 1-second Consistently window may flake on slow CI if reconcile latency spikes. The test author's inline DECISION comment explicitly acknowledges this trade-off (longer windows slow the suite; 1s catches near-immediate misfires). Consider making the window configurable via an env var or raising to 2s if CI flakes surface, but no change is required today.

The owner-reference fallback checks ref.Kind == "Keystone" with a hardcoded string literal, matching 6 other occurrences in the package. If the codebase later extracts a KeystoneKind constant, this call site should be updated alongside the others — not worth doing as part of this PR.

When the indexed List returns an error, the current implementation logs and swallows to keep the event queue flowing. Consider emitting a metric counter (e.g., keystone_secret_mapper_list_errors_total) so persistent indexer misconfiguration or API-server degradation becomes observable at the operator level. Not a blocker — log line is sufficient for now.

pattern_notes: This feature applies the standard controller-runtime FieldIndexer + EnqueueRequestsFromMapFunc idiom that is already used elsewhere in the ecosystem and documented in kubebuilder. It does not introduce a new project-specific pattern that warrants persistence: the index-key-as-exported-const, the extractor-function shape, and the registration-in-SetupWithManager placement all match upstream controller-runtime conventions. The pre-existing watch-handler structure and owner-reference-based dedup (CC-0081) are unchanged in spirit. Recommend no new pattern file.
